feedback.text: The need for age verification online is bigger everyday and I think that the Age Verification Blueprint is a step in the right direction, however it relies far too much on Google's services. This is problematic for two reasons. Firstly, because some of us would like to have as little to do with Google as possible. I personally do have a google account and email, but I plan on ridding myself of anything Google related and completely degoogling. I have taken steps in this direction and I worry, that I will be unable to access parts of the web if the age vetification blueprint, which I almost fully support, won't offer Google-free alternatives. Secondly, because Google is an american company and the EU should always strive to be less and less reliant on companies that aren't based in the EU. My wish for the people working on this act, is that you all have productive days and don't suffer too much pressure from above. Kind regards.
